rag_rules: AGREE contracts for component ports must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes
Implementation-level bindings and signal routing should be defined in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections are declared
generic_rules: Use assign statements only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation contracts
Component type contracts should relate visible outputs to visible inputs without referencing implementation-only subcomponent paths
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Ensure AGREE annex blocks are placed in valid AADL scopes corresponding to their semantic level (type vs. implementation)
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
anti_patterns: Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for output dependency contracts
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
